stem: A cloud computing server cluster uses 3 parallel nodes for redundancy. The request served requires at least one node to function. node 1 has a reliability of 97%. node 2 has a reliability of 78%. node 3 has a reliability between 67% and 78% (uncertain due to limited operational data). Additionally, there is a common-cause failure probability (e.g., power outage, shared software bug) that could disable all nodes simultaneously, estimated between 3.4% and 6.3%. What is the overall probability that the request served?

ground_truth.answer: P(request served) ranges from 0.9349 to 0.9646. Point estimate: 0.9493.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 nodes fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.03 × 0.22 × 0.33 = 0.0022. P(works) = 0.9978.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.03 × 0.22 × 0.22 = 0.0015. P(works) = 0.9985.
With common-cause failure (3.4%-6.3%): P(works) = (1-P_cc) × P(independent works). Range: [0.9349, 0.9646].
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
Ignoring common-cause failures that can disable all paths simultaneously
